events.title: Made in China 2025 Industrial Strategy Announced
events.detail: Premier Li Keqiang's State Council releases 'Made in China 2025' (中国制造2025), a 10-year industrial upgrading plan targeting 70% self-sufficiency in high-tech manufacturing by 2025. The plan designates 10 strategic sectors including semiconductors, aerospace, AI, and electric vehicles. It becomes the blueprint for China's technology independence ambitions and the central grievance in subsequent US-China trade disputes.

events.title: Internet Plus Action Plan Launched
events.detail: China's State Council unveils the 'Internet Plus' strategy, directing traditional industries to integrate internet technology into manufacturing, agriculture, finance, and public services. This initiative accelerates cloud computing, big data, and mobile internet adoption across China's economy. Alibaba, Tencent, and Baidu are positioned as implementation champions.

events.title: 13th Five-Year Plan Elevates AI and Robotics
events.detail: China's 13th Five-Year Plan (2016–2020) formally incorporates artificial intelligence, big data, and robotics as national strategic priorities. The plan allocates billions in state funding toward AI research institutions and calls for leading global innovation in key tech sectors. This sets the stage for China's 2017 New Generation AI Development Plan.

events.title: Alibaba Cloud Becomes World's Third-Largest Cloud Provider
events.detail: Alibaba Cloud surpasses IBM and Microsoft Azure in Asia-Pacific to become the world's third-largest cloud platform by revenue, trailing only AWS and Google Cloud. The milestone validates China's capacity to build globally competitive technology platforms. Alibaba doubles down on international expansion, opening data centers across Southeast Asia, Europe, and the Middle East.

events.title: New Generation AI Development Plan Released
events.detail: China's State Council releases the 'New Generation Artificial Intelligence Development Plan', setting ambitions to become the world's primary AI innovation center by 2030. The plan calls for a $150 billion domestic AI industry by 2030, identifies key applications in national defense, healthcare, smart cities, and agriculture. It reframes AI as a national security priority, not merely economic.

events.title: National Intelligence Law Enacted — Backdoor Fears Emerge
events.detail: China enacts the National Intelligence Law, legally obligating Chinese citizens and companies to 'support, assist, and cooperate' with national intelligence operations. Article 7 becomes the central basis for Western government concerns about Huawei equipment, TikTok data, and other Chinese technology platforms. The law reinforces existing Cybersecurity Law requirements for data localization and government access.

events.title: ZTE Pleads Guilty to Illegally Exporting US Technology to Iran
events.detail: Chinese telecom giant ZTE pleads guilty in US federal court to violating US export controls by shipping US technology to Iran and North Korea and then lying about it. ZTE agrees to pay $892 million in penalties. The case is a preview of the enforcement actions against Chinese tech firms that will follow, and demonstrates US willingness to use export controls as strategic tools.

events.title: Baidu Launches Apollo Autonomous Driving Platform
events.detail: Baidu launches Project Apollo, an open-source autonomous driving platform designed to become the 'Android of self-driving cars'. Over 100 global automakers and technology firms join the Apollo Alliance. The initiative demonstrates China's strategy of creating technology ecosystems to leverage network effects and establish standards in next-generation mobility before Western competitors can consolidate the market.

events.title: China Cybersecurity Law Takes Effect — Data Localization Mandated
events.detail: China's comprehensive Cybersecurity Law enters into force, requiring all 'critical information infrastructure' operators to store Chinese users' personal data and important business data within China. Foreign companies operating in China — including Apple, LinkedIn, and Google — must either localize data or exit the market. The law establishes the legal foundation for China's 'data sovereignty' regime and becomes a template for similar laws in Russia, India, and Vietnam.

events.title: Trump Blocks Broadcom's $117B Qualcomm Acquisition
events.detail: President Trump issues an executive order blocking Singapore-based Broadcom's hostile $117 billion takeover bid for Qualcomm, citing national security concerns. CFIUS warns that Chinese companies—particularly Huawei—could fill the market void if Qualcomm's R&D declined under new ownership. This is the largest CFIUS-blocked deal in history and signals a new era of technology M&A security screening.

events.title: US Bans American Companies from Supplying ZTE — Near-Death Blow
events.detail: The US Commerce Department bans American companies from selling components or software to ZTE for seven years, after discovering ZTE violated the terms of its 2017 plea agreement by paying bonuses to employees who violated sanctions rather than disciplining them. ZTE immediately stops major operations as 25-35% of its components come from US suppliers including Qualcomm and Intel. The ban nearly destroys ZTE until Trump intervenes.

events.title: ZTE Restored After $1.4B Fine and Executive Overhaul
events.detail: The US and ZTE reach a deal allowing ZTE to resume operations after paying $1.4 billion in fines and penalties, submitting to US compliance monitors embedded in ZTE's operations for 10 years, and replacing its entire board and senior management. The deal is criticized by US congressional leaders, but Trump uses it as a bargaining chip in US-China trade negotiations. The ZTE episode reveals both the power and limitations of US export controls.

events.title: FIRRMA Signed — CFIUS Powers Dramatically Expanded
events.detail: President Trump signs the Foreign Investment Risk Review Modernization Act (FIRRMA), the most significant expansion of the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) in decades. FIRRMA grants CFIUS jurisdiction over minority investments in US tech companies, real estate near sensitive facilities, and joint ventures. Chinese tech investment in the US drops 90% within two years as the law chills cross-border tech deals.

events.title: Huawei CFO Meng Wanzhou Arrested in Vancouver
events.detail: Huawei CFO and founder Ren Zhengfei's daughter Meng Wanzhou is arrested at Vancouver International Airport on a US extradition request, accused of bank fraud related to Huawei subsidiary Skycom's transactions with Iran. China demands her immediate release and arrests several Canadian citizens in apparent retaliation. The arrest triggers a diplomatic crisis that escalates US-China tensions and signals a new stage of confrontation over Huawei.

events.title: Huawei Added to US Entity List — Major Escalation
events.detail: The US Commerce Department adds Huawei and 68 affiliates to the Entity List, requiring US companies to obtain licenses before supplying Huawei with American technology, components, or software. Google immediately restricts Huawei's access to Android updates and Google services. Qualcomm, Intel, and Broadcom halt shipments. The action is the most consequential technology sanction in history by economic scale.

events.title: Huawei Mate 30 Pro Launches Without Google Services
events.detail: Huawei launches the Mate 30 Pro flagship smartphone without Google Play Store, Gmail, YouTube, or Google Maps, forced to use its own HMS (Huawei Mobile Services) ecosystem. Despite strong hardware specs from the TSMC-made Kirin 990 chip, sales outside China suffer dramatically. Huawei begins pouring resources into developing its own operating system HarmonyOS and AppGallery app marketplace.

events.title: China's National IC Fund Phase II Raises $29 Billion
events.detail: China establishes the second phase of its National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund (大基金 II), raising approximately 204.15 billion yuan ($29 billion). Phase II prioritizes upstream chipmaking equipment and materials — sectors heavily dependent on foreign suppliers like ASML, Applied Materials, and Tokyo Electron — reflecting lessons from the ZTE and Huawei crises about supply chain vulnerabilities.

events.title: TSMC Cuts Off Huawei Chip Supply After US Rule Change
events.detail: The US Commerce Department expands the Foreign Direct Product Rule (FDPR) to cover any chip made with US equipment or software anywhere in the world, directly targeting TSMC's ability to manufacture chips for Huawei's HiSilicon subsidiary. TSMC announces it will stop taking new Huawei orders by September 2020. This severs Huawei's access to advanced 5nm and 7nm chips, effectively crippling its smartphone and 5G businesses.

events.title: Kirin 9000 5G — Huawei's Last TSMC-Produced Chip
events.detail: TSMC completes its final production run of Huawei's Kirin 9000 5G chip (5nm process) before the US sanctions take full effect in September 2020. Huawei reportedly stockpiles as many chips as possible. The Kirin 9000 powers the Mate 40 series, which becomes the last Huawei flagship with a competitive, TSMC-manufactured processor for years.

events.title: Trump Issues Executive Orders Banning TikTok and WeChat
events.detail: President Trump issues executive orders requiring ByteDance to divest TikTok's US operations within 90 days and banning WeChat transactions in the US, citing national security concerns over Chinese access to American user data. ByteDance scrambles to negotiate a deal with Oracle and Walmart to host TikTok's US data. Courts temporarily block both orders before the Biden administration later withdraws them.

events.title: SMIC Added to US Entity List — China's Top Chipmaker Sanctioned
events.detail: The US Commerce Department adds Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC), China's largest chipmaker, to the Entity List, citing its 'military end use' risk. US companies must now obtain licenses — unlikely to be granted — to supply SMIC with equipment for chips at 10nm or more advanced nodes. SMIC loses access to cutting-edge EUV machines from ASML and advanced process equipment from Applied Materials.

events.title: DoJ Charges Huawei with Racketeering and IP Theft
events.detail: The US Department of Justice files a sweeping superseding indictment against Huawei, adding charges of racketeering conspiracy and conspiracy to steal trade secrets from six US companies including T-Mobile, alleging Huawei operated a decade-long global campaign to steal technology. The indictment names the Huawei corporate entity alongside two subsidiaries, expanding the legal pressure beyond the Meng Wanzhou extradition case.

events.title: Didi Delists from NYSE After CCP Pressure
events.detail: Chinese ride-hailing giant Didi announces it will delist from the New York Stock Exchange just five months after its $4.4 billion IPO, following intense pressure from Chinese regulators who launched a cybersecurity investigation immediately after the IPO. The Didi saga — combined with Alibaba's record $2.75 billion fine, Tencent's gaming restrictions, and ByteDance's education platform shutdown — marks the peak of Xi Jinping's sweeping tech crackdown.

events.title: Alibaba Fined Record $2.75 Billion in Antitrust Case
events.detail: China's State Administration for Market Regulation fines Alibaba a record 18.2 billion yuan ($2.75 billion) for antitrust violations, including forcing merchants to choose between Alibaba and rival platforms. The fine arrives amid Jack Ma's ongoing disappearance from public life following his October 2020 speech criticizing Chinese financial regulators. The episode signals Xi Jinping's determination to rein in the power of private technology companies.

sources.name: China State Administration for Market Regulation

events.title: CHIPS and Science Act Signed — $52.7B for US Semiconductor Industry
events.detail: President Biden signs the CHIPS and Science Act, authorizing $52.7 billion in federal investment in US semiconductor manufacturing ($39 billion) and R&D ($11 billion). The law aims to reshore chip production to the US after decades of offshoring to Asia. Guardrails prohibit CHIPS recipients from expanding advanced manufacturing in China for 10 years. Intel, TSMC, Samsung, and Micron all receive major grants under the program.

events.title: Biden's October 2022 Chip Export Controls — Most Sweeping Ever
events.detail: The Biden administration announces sweeping new export controls targeting China's ability to obtain advanced semiconductors, chip-making equipment, and supercomputing technology. The rules restrict exports of chips above 14nm for AI applications, advanced chipmaking equipment, and US persons working in China's semiconductor industry. Analysts call it the most significant US export control action since the Cold War, aiming to freeze China's semiconductor technology at least a generation behind the frontier.

events.title: ChatGPT Launches — Ignites Global AI Race with China
events.detail: OpenAI launches ChatGPT to the public, reaching one million users in five days and 100 million in two months — the fastest-growing consumer application in history. In Beijing, Chinese AI leaders panic that the US has pulled ahead dramatically. Baidu, Alibaba, Huawei, and dozens of Chinese startups immediately announce competing large language model programs, triggering what observers call China's 'Sputnik moment' in artificial intelligence.

events.title: China Issues First Generative AI Regulation Framework
events.detail: China's Cyberspace Administration releases draft regulations for 'Deep Synthesis Technology' (deepfakes and AI content), requiring AI-generated content to be labeled and prohibiting AI that undermines national security or spreads 'false information'. The rules are finalized in August 2023 as the 'Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services', making China the first major economy to regulate generative AI at scale.

events.title: Baidu ERNIE Bot Publicly Launches — China's First Major LLM
events.detail: Baidu opens ERNIE Bot (文心一言, Wenxin Yiyan) to the Chinese public, becoming the first major Chinese language model available at scale after months of closed testing. ERNIE Bot's underwhelming live demo caused Baidu's stock to drop 10%, but subsequent updates showed dramatic improvements. Baidu reports ERNIE Bot passes China's national college entrance exam (Gaokao) and medical licensing exams by mid-2023.

events.title: Huawei Mate 60 Pro with SMIC 7nm Chip Stuns US Officials
events.detail: Huawei quietly releases the Mate 60 Pro while Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo is visiting Beijing, featuring the Kirin 9000S chip manufactured by SMIC using a non-EUV 7nm-equivalent (N+2) process. TechInsights teardown confirms SMIC achieved 7nm-class transistor density without EUV lithography, using multiple patterning techniques. US officials are shocked — the advance suggests existing export controls failed to prevent China from reaching advanced nodes.

events.title: US Expands Chip Export Controls to Close Loopholes
events.detail: The Biden administration releases updated chip export controls to close loopholes that allowed Nvidia's A800 and H800 chips — specifically designed to remain just below the original October 2022 performance thresholds — to be sold in China. The new rules adopt a more holistic 'total processing performance' metric and expand restrictions to 40+ countries to prevent transshipment. Nvidia is estimated to lose billions in Chinese data center revenue.

events.title: YMTC and 35 More Chinese Firms Added to Entity List
events.detail: The US adds Yangtze Memory Technologies Corporation (YMTC), China's leading NAND flash memory producer, to the Entity List along with 35 other Chinese entities. YMTC had been Apple's planned supplier for iPhone NAND chips, but Apple drops the talks following US government pressure. The action cuts YMTC off from ASML, Applied Materials, and other equipment needed for next-generation 3D NAND production.

events.title: China Restricts Gallium and Germanium Exports — Retaliation
events.detail: China's Ministry of Commerce announces export restrictions on gallium and germanium compounds effective August 1, 2023, requiring export licenses for materials critical to semiconductor manufacturing, solar panels, and radar systems. China controls 80% of global gallium production and 60% of germanium. The restrictions are widely seen as retaliation for US chip export controls and a warning about Western supply chain vulnerabilities in critical minerals.

events.title: China's Big Fund Phase III Raises $47.5 Billion
events.detail: China establishes the third phase of the National Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund, raising 344 billion yuan ($47.5 billion) — the largest single tranche yet. Bringing the total government semiconductor investment across all phases to over $95 billion, Phase III focuses on equipment manufacturers, advanced packaging, and cutting-edge logic chips. The fund signals China's determination to achieve semiconductor self-sufficiency despite Western sanctions.

events.title: US Congress Passes TikTok Divest-or-Ban Law
events.detail: President Biden signs the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act, requiring ByteDance to divest TikTok's US operations within 270 days or face a ban. The law passes with overwhelming bipartisan support — 360-58 in the House, 79-18 in the Senate — after a classified national security briefing. ByteDance immediately challenges the law in federal courts, arguing it violates the First Amendment.

events.title: ASML Prohibited from Servicing Existing China EUV Machines
events.detail: The Dutch government expands export restrictions to prohibit ASML from providing servicing, maintenance, and software updates to EUV lithography machines already installed in China (mainly at SMIC). Without ongoing ASML support, EUV machines cannot reliably operate at full performance. The move closes a gap in previous restrictions that had allowed ASML to maintain existing machines even after blocking new EUV exports to China.

events.title: TikTok Goes Dark in US, Then Resumes After Trump Reprieve
events.detail: TikTok goes offline for US users at midnight on January 19, 2025, the day before the legal deadline, after the Supreme Court unanimously upholds the divest-or-ban law. The app goes dark for approximately 14 hours before President-elect Trump announces he will issue an executive order to pause enforcement after taking office on January 20. ByteDance and TikTok announce they are restoring service in the US, creating a 75-day de facto pause in the law.

events.title: DeepSeek V3 Released — Matches GPT-4o at Fraction of Cost
events.detail: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek releases DeepSeek-V3, a 671 billion parameter mixture-of-experts model that matches or exceeds GPT-4o on major benchmarks while reporting training costs of only ~2.788 million H800 GPU hours (approximately $5.6 million). DeepSeek claims the model was trained on Nvidia H800 chips (a downgraded version still available in China). The release shocks Silicon Valley, with investors questioning the business case for $100B+ AI infrastructure investments.

events.title: DeepSeek R1 Open-Sourced — Triggers Nvidia $600B Market Cap Crash
events.detail: DeepSeek releases R1, a reasoning model comparable to OpenAI's o1, as open-source on MIT license — free for anyone to download, modify, and deploy. R1 achieves 87.5% on AIME 2025 math benchmarks, competitive with leading Western models. Nvidia stock crashes 17% on January 27 — a $600 billion single-day market cap loss — the largest single-day market cap drop in US stock market history, as investors reassess the need for expensive AI chips.

events.title: Trump Administration Proposes TikTok Ownership Deal
events.detail: The Trump administration proposes a deal where TikTok's US operations would be split into a separate entity with 50% American ownership, potentially including Oracle, while ByteDance retains the algorithm. Trump's advisers float various structures including a joint venture with American investors. ByteDance and the Chinese government resist any deal that would require handing over the TikTok recommendation algorithm, which they consider proprietary technology and a strategic asset.

events.title: Huawei Ascend 910C AI Chips Deployed at Mass Scale
events.detail: Huawei's Ascend 910C AI training chip achieves mass deployment across Chinese hyperscalers, government data centers, and state-owned enterprises, positioning itself as the primary substitute for Nvidia H100s in China. Despite performance gaps versus the H100 (roughly 60-70% of comparable performance on some tasks), the Ascend 910C becomes the de facto standard for Chinese AI training. ByteDance, Baidu, and Alibaba all report large Huawei Ascend deployments.

events.title: China Accelerates AI Self-Sufficiency Industrial Policy
events.detail: China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology releases updated guidelines directing state-owned enterprises and government agencies to prioritize domestically made AI chips, AI frameworks (PaddlePaddle, MindSpore), and domestic large language models over foreign alternatives. The policy targets 80% domestic AI infrastructure by 2027. Chinese internet companies Baidu, Alibaba, and Huawei each announce multi-billion RMB AI infrastructure buildouts using domestic chips.

events.title: Huawei Pura 70 Launches with Fully Domestically Manufactured Chips
events.detail: Huawei releases the Pura 70 series featuring the Kirin 9010 chip, manufactured by SMIC using a refined N+2 7nm process with non-EUV techniques. The successful launch demonstrates that SMIC's 7nm capability shown in the Mate 60 Pro was not a one-off but a reproducible process at volume. Analysts estimate Huawei has recovered to produce over 30 million smartphones annually — a dramatic comeback from its 2021 nadir when it fell to ninth place globally.

events.title: US Restricts Nvidia H20 Chips to China — Final Market Closure
events.detail: The US Commerce Department announces export license requirements for Nvidia's H20 chip — previously downgraded specifically to comply with China export controls — effectively ending Nvidia's remaining legal AI chip sales to China. Nvidia takes a $4.5 billion inventory charge. The H20 had been a $12-15 billion annual revenue stream. The move signals US intent to deny China access to all high-performance AI computing hardware, including specially designed compliant versions.

events.title: China Leads Global Graphene Patent Applications for Next-Gen Chips
events.detail: Chinese universities and research institutions file the majority of global graphene-related semiconductor patents as China races to develop post-silicon chip technologies that would not be subject to Western export controls on traditional silicon-based equipment. Peking University and Chinese Academy of Sciences publish breakthrough research on carbon nanotube transistors and two-dimensional semiconductor materials. If successful, these could create an entirely new chipmaking paradigm where China's existing equipment can compete.

events.title: EU Imposes 35% Tariffs on Chinese EVs After Investigation
events.detail: The European Commission imposes additional tariffs of up to 35.3% on Chinese electric vehicles, on top of the existing 10% duty, following a year-long anti-subsidy investigation. BYD faces 17% additional tariff, Geely 19%, and SAIC 35.3%. China immediately challenges the tariffs at the WTO and threatens retaliatory measures on European luxury goods, cognac, and dairy products. Several EU member states including Germany vote against the tariffs, fearing Chinese retaliation.

events.title: China Restricts Graphite Exports — Battery Supply Chain at Risk
events.detail: China's Ministry of Commerce announces export controls on natural and synthetic graphite effective December 1, 2023, requiring export licenses for materials critical to EV battery anodes. China supplies 65% of natural graphite and 90%+ of battery-grade synthetic graphite globally. The restrictions are a direct counter to US EV battery supply chain diversification efforts and target a critical vulnerability in Western electric vehicle supply chains.

events.title: Meng Wanzhou Released After Deferred Prosecution Agreement
events.detail: Meng Wanzhou reaches a deferred prosecution agreement with the US Department of Justice admitting that Huawei misled banks about its Iran dealings, and is released from house arrest in Vancouver and returns to China. Canada simultaneously releases two Canadians — Michael Kovrig and Michael Spavor — who had been detained in apparent retaliation. The 'two Michaels' crisis underlines how Huawei's legal battles became entangled in a broader hostage-style diplomatic confrontation.

events.title: Multiple Labs Confirm SMIC Achieved 7nm Chips Without EUV
events.detail: Multiple independent semiconductor analysis firms including TechInsights, Chipworks, and SystemPlus Consulting confirm through teardowns that SMIC's Kirin 9000S uses a 7nm-equivalent FinFET process achieved through multi-patterning ArF immersion lithography without EUV machines. The finding suggests China's semiconductor progress under sanctions has exceeded US intelligence assessments. However, analysts note SMIC's yield rates are likely significantly lower than TSMC's, making mass production economically challenging.

events.title: China Bans Export of Seven Rare Earth Elements
events.detail: China's Ministry of Commerce announces export controls on seven critical rare earth elements including samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, and yttrium — materials essential for EV motors, military equipment, wind turbines, and semiconductor manufacturing. The restrictions escalate China's use of critical mineral dominance as leverage in the tech war, targeting supply chains for advanced weapons systems and clean energy technology.

events.title: TikTok US Divestiture Deal Finalized — Oracle, Silver Lake, MGX Acquire Stakes
events.detail: A final agreement resolving TikTok's US regulatory crisis was signed on January 22, 2026. Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX (Abu Dhabi sovereign fund) each acquired approximately 15% stakes in TikTok's US operations, while ByteDance retained a 19.9% stake. The deal reportedly included a $10 billion fee to the US Treasury, though Senator Mark Warner raised questions about the payment's structure. ByteDance's overall valuation reached $550 billion. The resolution ends a five-year saga: beginning with Trump's 2020 executive orders, the Supreme Court's January 2025 unanimous upholding of the divest-or-ban law, and a temporary blackout before a negotiated ownership restructuring superseded an outright ban.

events.title: China's 15th Five-Year Plan Passed — AI and Quantum Dominate National Strategy
events.detail: China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030) passed the National People's Congress on March 5, 2026, placing artificial intelligence at the center of national strategy — AI is mentioned 52 times, up from 11 in the 14th FYP. The plan introduces a sweeping 'AI+ action plan' targeting integration of AI across 90% of China's economy by 2030, with the digital economy reaching 12.5% of GDP. The new planning term 模芯云用 ('model-chip-cloud-application') appeared for the first time, reflecting a strategic pivot from chip manufacturing metrics to AI deployment and application metrics. Robotics and embodied AI received dedicated national standards, with China leading IEC global standards for elder-care robots. At least 7% average annual R&D spending growth is pledged, while quantum computing, 6G, and brain-computer interfaces are named as strategic future industries.

events.title: US DOJ Charges Super Micro Co-Founder with $2.5B China Chip Smuggling Scheme
events.detail: The US Department of Justice charged Super Micro co-founder Yih-Shyan 'Wally' Liaw and two associates with conspiring to smuggle approximately $2.5 billion in advanced Nvidia AI servers — including B200 and H200 GPUs — to China in violation of export controls. The indictment described an elaborate scheme using Southeast Asian intermediaries, falsified end-user certificates, and physical concealment including removal of serial numbers with heat guns. Super Micro shares fell more than 33%, erasing over $6 billion in market capitalization. Liaw resigned from the board the following day. The case represented the most significant US export control enforcement action since the CHIPS Act era, highlighting systematic enforcement gaps in restricting advanced AI chips from reaching China.

events.title: US Officials Allege SMIC Supplied Chipmaking Tools to Iran's Military
events.detail: Two senior US officials publicly alleged that SMIC, China's largest chip manufacturer, had been supplying chipmaking equipment and technical training to Iran's military for approximately one year. The officials did not specify whether US-origin technology was involved, which would represent a direct sanctions violation. SMIC has been on the US Entity List since December 2020 following the Huawei Kirin chip controversy. If confirmed, the transfers could expose SMIC to secondary sanctions that restrict its global market access and third-country equipment imports. The allegations add a new dimension to the US-China chip war: the possibility that China's sanctioned semiconductor infrastructure is being retransferred to US adversaries.

events.title: DeepSeek Grants Huawei Exclusive Early Access to V4 — China's AI Stack Integrates
events.detail: DeepSeek withheld its V4 model from Nvidia and AMD for optimization, granting exclusive early hardware access to Huawei and Cambricon — signaling an emerging China-domestic AI compute stack designed to function independently of US hardware. Huawei launched its UCM (Unified Cache Manager) system to reduce reliance on US-restricted High Bandwidth Memory chips. Tianjin Port's Second Container Terminal simultaneously demonstrated 88% process automation using Huawei PortGPT integrated with DeepSeek's LLM, up from 66% in 2024 — a milestone cited by Beijing as proof-of-concept for the 15th Five-Year Plan's 'AI+ action plan'. The Huawei-DeepSeek partnership represents China's strategy of co-designing model architectures with available domestic hardware to systematically close the performance gap imposed by US chip restrictions.

events.title: Huawei Launches Ascend 950PR — ByteDance and Alibaba Place First Orders
events.detail: Huawei unveiled the Ascend 950PR AI chip, its most advanced inference accelerator, with ByteDance and Alibaba immediately placing substantial orders following successful testing. The 950PR improves CUDA ecosystem compatibility — removing the primary adoption barrier that had limited Huawei chip uptake at Chinese hyperscalers with existing CUDA-based model pipelines. Huawei plans to ship approximately 750,000 units in 2026, with mass production from April and volume deliveries in H2 2026. Pricing starts at approximately 50,000 yuan ($6,900). The launch marks a critical milestone in China's domestic AI compute transition: Chinese hyperscalers that previously resisted migrating from Nvidia's CUDA ecosystem can now deploy Huawei chips without full software stack rewrites. Each 950PR delivers inference performance estimated at 70-80% of an Nvidia H100, up from ~60% for the Ascend 910C, narrowing the gap at a time when Nvidia access is fully restricted.

events.title: SMIC Posts Record $9.33B Revenue for 2025; Plans Above-Industry Growth in 2026
events.detail: SMIC reported record full-year 2025 revenue of $9.327 billion — up 20.9% year-over-year — with net profit growing 39% to $685 million. The results confirm SMIC's position as the undisputed leader of China's domestic semiconductor foundry industry. The 2026 action plan maintains capital expenditure at ~$8.1 billion focused on legacy-node expansion in BCD, analog, MCU, and display driver IC processes. Despite being on the US Entity List since December 2020, SMIC has grown revenue by approximately 200% over five years, driven by China's domestic chip demand surge following US sanctions. TrendForce simultaneously projected that China's domestic AI chip market share will reach 50% in 2026, driven by SMIC-manufactured Huawei Ascend chips. The record results arrived amid unverified US allegations that SMIC transferred chipmaking equipment to Iran's military — allegations Beijing dismissed as 'false information' on March 29.

events.title: China's Core AI Industry Exceeds 1.2 Trillion Yuan; 6,200+ AI Enterprises Nationwide
events.detail: Xinhua reported that China's core AI industry value exceeded 1.2 trillion yuan (~$174 billion) at end-2025, with more than 6,200 AI enterprises operating nationwide. The 15th Five-Year Plan's 2026 implementation guidelines direct a push to deepen AI integration into traditional manufacturing sectors and SMEs — identifying production-line deployment as the key lagging area. China's AI market is forecast to exceed 1.73 trillion yuan by 2028. The Xiong'an 'AI+' Industry Forum (April 1) unveiled R&D incentives and GPU cluster subsidies to accelerate the AI-industrial integration. China targets the digital economy reaching 12.5% of GDP by 2030. The figures position China as the world's second-largest AI market by industry value; the US maintains a significant lead in frontier model investment at $109.1 billion private AI investment in 2024 versus China's $9.3 billion.

events.title: DeepSeek V4 Launches on Huawei Ascend — China's Full-Stack AI Independence Validated
events.detail: DeepSeek released its V4 model preview on April 24, 2026 — one year after R1 disrupted global AI markets. V4-Pro features 1.6 trillion parameters, a 1 million token context window, and validated native compatibility with Huawei Ascend NPUs, marking a decisive step in China's CUDA-independence push. Huawei confirmed Ascend 950 chips fully support V4 through its 'Supernode' cluster technology. API pricing reflected a ~73% inference cost reduction vs V3. SMIC shares surged 9% and Hua Hong Semiconductor 15% on the news. The launch arrived one day after Commerce Secretary Lutnick confirmed zero Nvidia H200 chip purchases by China — together validating China's deliberate strategy of building competitive AI capability entirely within the domestic hardware ecosystem. DeepSeek remains fully open-source; Tencent and Alibaba simultaneously entered talks to invest at a $20B+ valuation. At Auto China 2026 opening the same day, Xpeng unveiled a flying car, IRON humanoid robot, and GX Level 4 robotaxi powered by in-house Turing AI chips — crystallizing China's transition to a fully integrated AI-hardware-mobility platform competitor.

media.caption: DeepSeek V4 launches on Huawei Ascend — China's AI independence stack validated one year after R1 shocked global markets
